headlines.title: Trump slams NATO over lax participation in Iran war in talk with Mark Rutte
headlines.snippet: NATO chief Mark Rutte highlighted the use of US bases in Europe while stressing support for the US war on Iran. He made these points during a conversation with Trump, who had criticized NATO's participation.

headlines.title: Trump White House requests $87.6bn in spending, including for Iran war
headlines.snippet: The White House has requested $87.6 billion in additional spending, with a significant portion allocated for the war on Iran. This request faces challenges in Congress, which recently voted to limit Trump's war powers.

headlines.title: European heatwave, scorching weather triggers UK ‘red’ warning
headlines.snippet: Britain has issued a 'red' warning as a severe heatwave spreads across Europe, bringing record-breaking June temperatures. The scorching weather is impacting the UK and other parts of the continent.

headlines.title: Katz: Israel will not withdraw from Lebanon even if US demands it
headlines.snippet: Defence Minister Israel Katz stated that Israel will maintain its presence in Lebanon. This decision stands even if the United States demands a withdrawal.

headlines.title: Palestinian activist faints after his release from Israeli prison
headlines.snippet: Palestinian activist Abdullah Shatat collapsed shortly after being released from Israeli prison. The incident occurred as he was regaining his freedom.

headlines.title: Trump criticises European allies over lack of support during war on Iran
headlines.snippet: Trump criticized several European allies for their perceived lack of support during the US-Israeli war on Iran. He mentioned that while the US didn't require assistance, stronger backing would have been appreciated.

headlines.title: Trump casts doubt on US responsibility for deadly Iran school strike
headlines.snippet: Trump expressed doubt about US responsibility for a deadly school strike in Iran, stating he had seen no evidence implicating the United States. This occurred on the first day of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

headlines.title: Vance says US reviewing conditions for Türkiye's F-35 purchase
headlines.snippet: US Vice President JD Vance is reviewing the conditions for Türkiye's F-35 purchase with the Pentagon. The review will determine if Türkiye has met the legal requirements stipulated by American law.

headlines.title: UN nuclear chief says inspections in Iran will go ahead despite Tehran's caution
headlines.snippet: The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) confirmed that UN nuclear inspectors will proceed with inspections at Iran's enrichment sites. This will happen despite Tehran's indication that inspections are conditional on a final agreement.

headlines.title: Israeli army says it struck two people in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The Israeli military announced it struck two individuals in southern Lebanon. The army claimed they posed a threat to its forces while traveling in a vehicle in the occupied area.

headlines.title: Al Arabiya correspondent Mohammed Aydah killed in explosion in Yemen’s Mukalla
headlines.snippet: Al Arabiya correspondent Mohammed Aydah was killed in an explosion that occurred in Mukalla, Yemen.

headlines.title: Federal judge bars Trump from implementing proof of citizenship requirement to vote
headlines.snippet: A federal judge has issued an order preventing Trump from enforcing a proof of citizenship requirement for voting.

headlines.title: US-Iran deal may leave Netanyahu as biggest casualty
headlines.snippet: The deal between the US and Iran may result in Netanyahu being the most significant casualty.

headlines.title: Italy slams NATO chief’s comments on Iran war flights
headlines.snippet: Italy has officially refuted comments made by NATO chief Rutte concerning Iran war flights.

headlines.title: Russian strike on Ukraine kills mine disposal expert: NGO
headlines.snippet: A mine disposal expert was killed in Ukraine as a result of a Russian strike, according to an NGO.

headlines.title: Trump seeks $88 bn in extra funding, mostly for Iran war
headlines.snippet: The White House has requested $88 billion in supplemental funding, with the majority designated for the war on Iran.

headlines.title: Colombia president-elect to work with Israel 'like never before'
headlines.snippet: The president-elect of Colombia has stated his intention to work with Israel more closely than ever before.

headlines.title: UK government's answer to stolen Palestinian land? Ad watchdog
headlines.snippet: The UK government's response to the sale of stolen Palestinian land has been through its advertising watchdog. This highlights the perceived weakness of recent UK government sanctions.

headlines.title: Syria trial opens for Assad cousin accused of war crimes
headlines.snippet: A trial has commenced in Syria for an individual accused of war crimes. The court is considering acts attributed to Wassim al-Assad as crimes against humanity.

headlines.title: US to progress with Turkey jet engine sales ahead of NATO summit
headlines.snippet: The United States is moving forward with jet engine sales to Turkey ahead of a NATO summit. Relations between Turkey and the US have been generally warm.

headlines.title: مصدر أمني للميادين: تم تجهيز مقدمات جدول انسحاب الاحتلال من جنوب لبنان وهو قيد الدراسة
headlines.snippet: An security source stated that preliminary steps for the occupation's withdrawal from southern Lebanon have been prepared and are under study.
